senators.name: Dick Durbin
senators.nerdReason: B.S.F.S. from Georgetown University School of Foreign Service and J.D. from Georgetown University Law Center. Senate's second-longest-serving member. As Senate Majority Whip and senior Judiciary Committee member, demonstrates deep procedural and constitutional expertise. Known for meticulous legislative craftsmanship and detailed policy analysis.
education: B.S.F.S., Georgetown University School of Foreign Service

senators.name: Tammy Duckworth
senators.nerdReason: B.A. in political science from University of Hawaii, M.A. in international affairs from George Washington University, and Ph.D. in human services from Capella University. Army Black Hawk helicopter pilot who lost both legs in Iraq. A doctorate combined with military analytical expertise and policy depth. Applies research rigor to veterans affairs, defense, and disability policy.
education: M.A. International Affairs, George Washington University
B.A. Political Science, University of Hawaii at Manoa
experience: U.S. Army Lieutenant Colonel (ret.), helicopter pilot
Assistant Secretary, U.S. Department of Veterans Affairs

IL-01.name: Jonathan Jackson
IL-01.notNerdReason: B.A. from North Carolina A&T State University. Career in business and civic engagement. No analytical, technical, or research-based policy background.
education: B.A., North Carolina A&T State University

IL-02.name: Robin Kelly
IL-02.nerdReason: B.A. from Bradley University, M.A. and Ph.D. in political science from Northern Illinois University. A doctorate demonstrates deep research methodology and analytical rigor. Applied policy expertise as Chief of Staff to Illinois State Treasurer and as Chief Administrative Officer of Cook County.
education: Ph.D. Political Science, Northern Illinois University
M.A. Political Science, Northern Illinois University
experience: Chief of Staff, Illinois State Treasurer

IL-03.name: Delia Ramirez
IL-03.notNerdReason: B.A. from Northern Illinois University. Career in community organizing and nonprofit leadership. Policy driven by community experience and advocacy rather than analytical or research-based frameworks.

IL-04.name: Jesus Garcia
IL-04.notNerdReason: B.A. from University of Illinois Chicago. Career in community organizing, social work, and local politics. Deep community engagement but approach driven by grassroots experience rather than analytical policy frameworks.

IL-05.name: Mike Quigley
IL-05.nerdReason: B.A. from Roosevelt University, M.P.A. from University of Chicago, and J.D. from Loyola University Chicago. Three post-secondary degrees spanning public administration, law, and political science. University of Chicago MPA emphasizes quantitative policy analysis. Career as Cook County commissioner and transparency advocate demonstrates data-driven governance approach.

IL-06.name: Sean Casten
IL-06.nerdReason: B.A. in molecular biology and biochemistry from Middlebury College. M.S. in biochemical engineering and M.E.M. from Dartmouth's Thayer School of Engineering. Worked as a scientist at Tufts School of Medicine researching cancer. CEO of clean energy companies (Turbosteam, Recycled Energy Development). Key author of the Inflation Reduction Act. One of Congress's foremost experts on climate science and energy policy, grounding legislation in engineering and scientific data.
education: M.S. Biochemical Engineering, Dartmouth (Thayer School of Engineering)
B.A. Molecular Biology and Biochemistry, Middlebury College
experience: Research Scientist, Tufts School of Medicine

IL-07.name: Danny Davis
IL-07.nerdReason: B.A. from Arkansas AM&N College, M.S. from Chicago State University, and Ph.D. from Union Institute & University. A doctorate demonstrates scholarly commitment. Career in health and human services administration and teaching. Combines academic credentials with decades of deep policy expertise in poverty, criminal justice reform, and healthcare.

IL-08.name: Raja Krishnamoorthi
IL-08.nerdReason: B.S.E. in mechanical engineering summa cum laude from Princeton University with a certificate from the Princeton School of Public and International Affairs. J.D. with honors from Harvard Law School. Combines elite engineering and legal analytical training. President of research-oriented small businesses in national security and renewable energy technology. Ranking Member of the Select Committee on China. One of the most technically credentialed members of Congress.
education: B.S.E. Mechanical Engineering (summa cum laude), Princeton University
experience: Ranking Member, Select Committee on China
Special Assistant Attorney General, Illinois Public Integrity Unit

IL-09.name: Jan Schakowsky
IL-09.notNerdReason: B.A. from University of Illinois. Career in consumer advocacy and state legislature. Long-tenured on Energy and Commerce Committee with strong policy engagement. Driven by consumer protection values rather than analytical frameworks.
experience: Program Director, Illinois Public Action Council

IL-10.name: Brad Schneider
IL-10.nerdReason: B.S. in industrial engineering from Northwestern University and M.B.A. from Northwestern's Kellogg School of Management. Engineering degree provides strong analytical and quantitative foundation. Career as management consultant and industrial engineer. Describes his engineering training as shaping how he approaches cause-and-effect in policymaking. Endorsed by 314 Action (STEM advocacy PAC).

IL-11.name: Bill Foster
IL-11.nerdReason: B.S. in physics from University of Wisconsin-Madison and Ph.D. in physics from Harvard University. Particle physicist at Fermi National Accelerator Laboratory (Fermilab) for 22 years. Member of the team that discovered the top quark. Designed and built particle accelerators and scientific detectors. The only Ph.D. physicist in Congress. Brings deep scientific methodology and evidence-based reasoning to energy, technology, and financial policy.

IL-12.name: Mike Bost
IL-12.notNerdReason: Attended University of Illinois Certified Firefighters Academy. U.S. Marine Corps veteran. Career as firefighter and state legislator. No college degree or analytical/research-based policy background.
education: University of Illinois Certified Firefighters Academy

IL-13.name: Nikki Budzinski
IL-13.notNerdReason: B.A. from George Mason University. Career in labor policy and government. Served as senior advisor in the Biden White House Office of Management and Budget. OMB experience shows budget and data engagement, but overall career is in political operations and labor advocacy rather than analytical rigor.
experience: Senior Advisor, White House Office of Management and Budget
Chief of Staff, Illinois Governor's Office

IL-14.name: Lauren Underwood
IL-14.nerdReason: B.S. in nursing from University of Michigan, M.S. in nursing and M.P.H. from Johns Hopkins University. Registered nurse with dual master's degrees from a world-class public health institution. Served as senior advisor at HHS during the ACA implementation. Applies clinical nursing expertise and public health research methodology to healthcare legislation. Teaches at Georgetown University School of Nursing.
experience: Senior Advisor, U.S. Department of Health and Human Services
Adjunct Instructor, Georgetown University School of Nursing

IL-15.name: Mary Miller
IL-15.notNerdReason: B.A. from Eastern Illinois University. Career as farmer and homemaker before Congress. No analytical, technical, or evidence-based policy background. Legislative approach driven by ideology rather than data or expertise.

IL-16.name: Darin LaHood
IL-16.notNerdReason: B.A. from Loras College and J.D. from John Marshall Law School. Career as federal prosecutor and state senator. Son of former Transportation Secretary Ray LaHood. Legal and prosecutorial background shows analytical skills but governance approach is not distinctly research-driven or evidence-based.

IL-17.name: Eric Sorensen
IL-17.nerdReason: B.A. from University of Iowa. Career as TV meteorologist for 22 years, requiring understanding of atmospheric science, data interpretation, and climate patterns. Became a leading voice on climate change in Congress, grounding arguments in meteorological expertise. First openly gay member of Congress from Illinois. Science communication background gives him unique data-literacy among members.
